circular.title: Guidelines on Digital Lending
circular.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has issued guidelines on digital lending to manage risks and protect customers. The guidelines apply to all regulated entities and cover aspects such as customer protection, conduct issues, and technology and data requirements. The guidelines aim to ensure that digital lending is carried out in a fair and transparent manner.

clauses.text: Provide a framework for managing risks in digital lending.
clauses.summary: Framework for managing risks in digital lending

clauses.text: These guidelines applies to all Regulated Entities (REs).
clauses.summary: Applicability of guidelines to Regulated Entities

clauses.text: Digital Lending: A remote and automated lending process, largely by use of seamless digital technologies.
Lending Service Provider (LSP): An agent of a Regulated Entity who carries out one or more of lender’s functions.
All loan dispersals and repayments shall be executed only between the bank accounts of borrower and the RE without any pass-through or pool account of the LSP.
Any fees, charges, etc., payable to LSPs shall be paid directly by the RE and not by the borrower.
clauses.summary: Payment of fees and charges to LSPs

clauses.text: Penal interest/charges levied, if any, on the borrowers shall be based on the outstanding amount of the loan, and kept transparent.
If a RE fails to disclose the Key Fact Statement (KFS) upfront, a penalty of ₹10,000 per violation shall be imposed.
clauses.summary: Penalty for non-disclosure of Key Fact Statement

clauses.text: REs shall ensure that LSPs engaged by them do not store personal information of borrowers except some basic minimal data (viz., name, address, contact details of the customer).
This circular modifies the Fair Practices Code guidelines.
clauses.summary: Modification of Fair Practices Code guidelines

obligations.text: Provide a framework for managing risks in digital lending.
obligations.plain_english: Regulated entities must have a framework to manage risks in digital lending.

obligations.text: All loan dispersals and repayments shall be executed only between the bank accounts of borrower and the RE without any pass-through or pool account of the LSP.
obligations.plain_english: Loans must be disbursed and repaid directly between the borrower and the regulated entity, without using the LSP's account.

obligations.text: Any fees, charges, etc., payable to LSPs shall be paid directly by the RE and not by the borrower.
obligations.plain_english: Regulated entities must pay fees and charges to LSPs directly, and not pass them on to the borrower.

obligations.text: Penal interest/charges levied, if any, on the borrowers shall be based on the outstanding amount of the loan, and kept transparent.
obligations.plain_english: Any penal interest or charges levied on borrowers must be based on the outstanding loan amount and be transparent.

obligations.text: REs shall ensure that LSPs engaged by them do not store personal information of borrowers except some basic minimal data (viz., name, address, contact details of the customer).
obligations.plain_english: Regulated entities must ensure that LSPs do not store personal information of borrowers, except for basic data such as name, address, and contact details.

prohibitions.text: LSPs shall not store personal information of borrowers except some basic minimal data (viz., name, address, contact details of the customer).
prohibitions.plain_english: LSPs are not allowed to store personal information of borrowers, except for basic data such as name, address, and contact details.

penalties.condition: Failure to disclose Key Fact Statement upfront

definitions.definition: A remote and automated lending process, largely by use of seamless digital technologies.
definitions.plain_english: Digital lending refers to the use of technology to provide loans remotely and automatically.

definitions.definition: An agent of a Regulated Entity who carries out one or more of lender’s functions.
definitions.plain_english: A Lending Service Provider is an agent of a regulated entity that performs certain functions on behalf of the lender.
